sections.executive_summary:
This proposal covers a two-phase AI workflow automation engagement for [Company Name]: first a structured audit to identify and prioritize automation opportunities, then implementation of the highest-value workflows. Based on our initial conversation, we expect to find 15–25 hours of automatable work per week across your operations. The goal is to implement the top 5 automations and deliver a roadmap for the rest.

Project investment: $5,500. Timeline: 6 weeks.
sections.problem_statement:
[Company Name] has grown to [X] people processing significant operational volume — [describe the operational context]. Much of this work involves moving information between systems, following up on outstanding items, generating regular documents, or triggering downstream tasks based on upstream events. These aren't complex decisions — they're repetitive actions that happen the same way every time.

The cost isn't just in the hours. It's in the errors that manual processes introduce. It's in the delays when someone is out sick. It's in the institutional knowledge that lives in one person's head about when to do what. And it's in the growth ceiling: your current processes won't scale past [X] without either hiring significantly more people or automating.
sections.proposed_solution:
**Phase 1: Automation Audit (Week 1)**

We run a structured 3-hour workshop with your operations team to map how work actually flows through the business. We're looking for:
- Repetitive trigger-action patterns (if X happens, do Y)
- Manual data movement between systems
- Recurring document or report generation
- Approval and routing workflows
- Monitoring and alerting tasks

Output: An Automation Opportunity Report with every opportunity ranked by three factors: time savings per week, implementation complexity, and error reduction impact. You get to see the full picture and decide which ones to build first.

**Phase 2: Implementation (Weeks 2–6)**

We build the top 5 automations identified in the audit. Each automation includes:
- Documented logic (what triggers it, what it does, what happens on exceptions)
- Testing with real data
- Monitoring so you know when it runs and when it doesn't
- Documentation for your team to understand and maintain it

Typical automations we build in this type of engagement:
- New lead → CRM record created + assigned + welcome email sent
- Invoice received → categorized + approval routed + payment scheduled
- Project milestone reached → next task assigned + client notified
- Weekly report generated automatically from live data
- Form submission → document created + filed + follow-up scheduled
sections.deliverables_and_timeline:
**Week 1:**
- 3-hour automation audit workshop
- Automation Opportunity Report (all opportunities ranked)
- Implementation priority list

**Weeks 2–3:**
- Automations 1–2 built and tested
- Documentation written

**Weeks 4–5:**
- Automations 3–5 built and tested
- Integration testing across workflows

**Week 6:**
- Team training (1-hour session + recorded walkthrough)
- Final documentation package
- Handover call
- 30-day check-in scheduled
sections.investment:
**Project Total: $5,500**

Includes:
- Automation audit and opportunity report
- 5 automation builds
- All integrations (up to 8 platform connections total)
- Documentation for each automation
- Team training
- 30-day post-handover support

**Additional automations beyond 5:** $800 each. **Additional platform connections beyond 8:** $400 each.

**Payment:** $2,750 to start (covers audit + first 2 automations), $2,750 upon project completion.

**The math:** If we find 20 hours/week of automatable work and implement automations covering half of that, you save 10 hours/week at $30/hour fully loaded = $1,300/month. This project pays for itself in 4 months — and the automations run indefinitely.
sections.team: [Your Name] as lead automation architect. Tools expertise: Make (Integromat), Zapier, n8n, Airtable, Notion, Python for custom logic. References from comparable engagements available.
sections.next_steps:
1. Confirm project start date
2. Sign service agreement
3. Pay first installment ($2,750)
4. List your key tools and systems (we'll send a prep form) — 15 minutes to complete
5. Automation audit workshop scheduled for week 1: [proposed date/time]

The prep form helps us hit the ground running in the audit session — we arrive knowing your tools and can dive straight into the process mapping.

sections.executive_summary:
This proposal covers workflow automation specifically for [Agency Name]'s client delivery operations — from onboarding to reporting to offboarding. We'll automate the repeatable parts of your client workflow so your team focuses on the work that actually requires their expertise.

Project investment: $4,000. Timeline: 4 weeks.
sections.problem_statement: Agency operations have a specific set of recurring workflows: client onboarding (contracts, kickoff scheduling, access collection, project setup), weekly/monthly reporting, invoice generation, project status updates. These tasks happen the same way for every client — yet they're being done manually every time, by people who have more valuable work to do.
sections.proposed_solution:
Build an agency operations automation layer:
1. Client onboarding: new client → contract sent → project space created → kickoff invite sent → intake form triggered → access credentials requested — all automatically, triggered by a single action.
2. Reporting: automated data pull + report generation + email delivery on schedule.
3. Invoice generation: triggered by billing date or milestone, created from template, sent automatically.
4. Project status: weekly internal digest automatically compiled from project management tool data.
sections.deliverables_and_timeline: 4 weeks. Covers all four automation categories above. Includes documentation and team training.
sections.next_steps: Share your current project management tool, CRM, and billing system. Map out your current onboarding checklist. Schedule kickoff.
